question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . 3) External partners shown as Offline / late chats / calls not ringing: - The Teams/Direct Routing incident where external signalling and media were impacted after a firewall/security change shows comparable symptoms (media/presence/chat delays can be observed when firewall or media flow is altered). context: SOURCE 5: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls Ticket number: TI-1602241 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 14 Owner: Employee 33 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 08.07.25 08:21 Closure Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05 Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again. Activities 08.07.25 08:10 Call from Mr. Contact Person 14. He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”. Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected. 08.07.25 08:21 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33. 08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14 Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14 Thank you for your report. Could you please briefly confirm the following points: – Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)? – Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected? – Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones? – Since when has the issue existed? Thank you for your feedback. Kind regards 08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14 Good morning Yes, it affects only external calls. Both incoming and outgoing. At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location. Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday. Kind regards 08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note Test call performed with user S. Meier. Internal Teams call works. External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing. Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message. 08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test. Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical? 08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response We tested with three additional persons. Same behavior everywhere. Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly). LAN connection. Home office users do not seem to be affected. 09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again Good morning We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend. Could this have an impact? Kind regards 09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note Firewall change timing plausible. Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team. 09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18 09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration. Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established. We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream). We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings. Kind regards 10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis SBC shows successful SIP connections. No call drops. Media negotiation (SDP) is performed. Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification. Voice transmission is now stable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?

context: SOURCE 16: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds Ticket number: TI-1604879 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 16 Owner: Employee 35 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 22.07.25 09:14 Closure Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52 Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable. Activities 22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk Good morning Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds. The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time. Internal Teams calls are not affected. Could you please review this? Kind regards 22.07.25 09:14 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35. 22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Thank you for your report. Could you please confirm: – Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls? – Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration? – Are all locations affected or only a specific one? Thank you very much. 22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response It only affects the Basel location. Both incoming and outgoing calls. The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier. Home office users are not affected. 22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. External call to mobile phone. Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds. No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.” 23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis Possible client error or unstable connection. Test with another device → same behavior. Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change. 23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs. Signaling is established correctly. The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client. Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location? Kind regards 23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented. A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created. Could this be relevant? 24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis SBC logs reviewed: – SIP INVITE successful – 200 OK correct – Call established – After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC No obvious error code. 24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis No blocking visible. Ports open according to standard configuration. 24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis Session-Expires header reviewed. Session timer set to 30 seconds. Comparison with other locations shows: There, 180 seconds. 25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation Document: “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1” Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant. Note in the blueprint: For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination. 25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC. Test call performed. Call remains stable for several minutes. Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible. 28.07.25 – Coordination with network team New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy. UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds. Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds. 29.07.25 – Adjustment NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 30.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We conducted several test calls. No more disconnections observed. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you very much. 31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 16 The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Calls now remain stable. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

context: SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? context: SOURCE 5: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls Ticket number: TI-1602241 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 14 Owner: Employee 33 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 08.07.25 08:21 Closure Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05 Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again. Activities 08.07.25 08:10 Call from Mr. Contact Person 14. He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”. Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected. 08.07.25 08:21 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33. 08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14 Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14 Thank you for your report. Could you please briefly confirm the following points: – Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)? – Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected? – Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones? – Since when has the issue existed? Thank you for your feedback. Kind regards 08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14 Good morning Yes, it affects only external calls. Both incoming and outgoing. At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location. Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday. Kind regards 08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note Test call performed with user S. Meier. Internal Teams call works. External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing. Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message. 08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test. Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical? 08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response We tested with three additional persons. Same behavior everywhere. Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly). LAN connection. Home office users do not seem to be affected. 09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again Good morning We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend. Could this have an impact? Kind regards 09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note Firewall change timing plausible. Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team. 09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18 09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration. Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established. We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream). We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings. Kind regards 10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis SBC shows successful SIP connections. No call drops. Media negotiation (SDP) is performed. Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification. Voice transmission is now stable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. context: SOURCE 5: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls Ticket number: TI-1602241 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 14 Owner: Employee 33 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 08.07.25 08:21 Closure Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05 Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again. Activities 08.07.25 08:10 Call from Mr. Contact Person 14. He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”. Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected. 08.07.25 08:21 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33. 08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14 Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14 Thank you for your report. Could you please briefly confirm the following points: – Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)? – Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected? – Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones? – Since when has the issue existed? Thank you for your feedback. Kind regards 08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14 Good morning Yes, it affects only external calls. Both incoming and outgoing. At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location. Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday. Kind regards 08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note Test call performed with user S. Meier. Internal Teams call works. External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing. Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message. 08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test. Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical? 08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response We tested with three additional persons. Same behavior everywhere. Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly). LAN connection. Home office users do not seem to be affected. 09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again Good morning We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend. Could this have an impact? Kind regards 09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note Firewall change timing plausible. Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team. 09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18 09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration. Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established. We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream). We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings. Kind regards 10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis SBC shows successful SIP connections. No call drops. Media negotiation (SDP) is performed. Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification. Voice transmission is now stable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 16: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds Ticket number: TI-1604879 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 16 Owner: Employee 35 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 22.07.25 09:14 Closure Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52 Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable. Activities 22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk Good morning Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds. The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time. Internal Teams calls are not affected. Could you please review this? Kind regards 22.07.25 09:14 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35. 22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Thank you for your report. Could you please confirm: – Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls? – Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration? – Are all locations affected or only a specific one? Thank you very much. 22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response It only affects the Basel location. Both incoming and outgoing calls. The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier. Home office users are not affected. 22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. External call to mobile phone. Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds. No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.” 23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis Possible client error or unstable connection. Test with another device → same behavior. Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change. 23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs. Signaling is established correctly. The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client. Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location? Kind regards 23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented. A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created. Could this be relevant? 24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis SBC logs reviewed: – SIP INVITE successful – 200 OK correct – Call established – After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC No obvious error code. 24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis No blocking visible. Ports open according to standard configuration. 24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis Session-Expires header reviewed. Session timer set to 30 seconds. Comparison with other locations shows: There, 180 seconds. 25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation Document: “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1” Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant. Note in the blueprint: For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination. 25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC. Test call performed. Call remains stable for several minutes. Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible. 28.07.25 – Coordination with network team New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy. UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds. Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds. 29.07.25 – Adjustment NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 30.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We conducted several test calls. No more disconnections observed. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you very much. 31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 16 The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Calls now remain stable. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

context: SOURCE 5: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls Ticket number: TI-1602241 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 14 Owner: Employee 33 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 08.07.25 08:21 Closure Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05 Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again. Activities 08.07.25 08:10 Call from Mr. Contact Person 14. He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”. Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected. 08.07.25 08:21 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33. 08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14 Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14 Thank you for your report. Could you please briefly confirm the following points: – Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)? – Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected? – Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones? – Since when has the issue existed? Thank you for your feedback. Kind regards 08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14 Good morning Yes, it affects only external calls. Both incoming and outgoing. At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location. Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday. Kind regards 08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note Test call performed with user S. Meier. Internal Teams call works. External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing. Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message. 08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test. Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical? 08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response We tested with three additional persons. Same behavior everywhere. Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly). LAN connection. Home office users do not seem to be affected. 09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again Good morning We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend. Could this have an impact? Kind regards 09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note Firewall change timing plausible. Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team. 09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18 09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration. Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established. We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream). We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings. Kind regards 10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis SBC shows successful SIP connections. No call drops. Media negotiation (SDP) is performed. Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification. Voice transmission is now stable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

Kind regards 22.07.25 09:14 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35. 22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Thank you for your report. Kind regards 23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented. A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created. Comparison with other locations shows: There, 180 seconds. 25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation Document: “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1” Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant. Note in the blueprint: For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination. 25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC. UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds. Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds. 29.07.25 – Adjustment NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 30.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We conducted several test calls. No more disconnections observed. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you very much. 31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 16 The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Calls now remain stable. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

context: SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

Final recommendation (short) - Treat this as a single high‑priority, multi‑service incident and escalate to 2nd‑level / specialist teams now (Network/Security, UC, ERP, Print). The dataset shows the same class of cross‑area outages in the past required those teams and configuration changes (firewall/media ports, session timers, print VLAN routing, Data Mart resets / AOS restarts) to resolve
